reasoning: The forecast requires comparing the daily average temperature in Bourges, France on three future dates (Nov 16, Dec 9, Feb 7) against a fixed cutoff date (Nov 9). In Bourges, average daily temperatures drop rapidly throughout November (from ~8.6°C on Nov 9 to ~7.6°C early/mid-month), reach their lowest point in January (~4.2°C), and slowly start rising in February. Since the temperature on Nov 9 is unknown but expected to follow this climatology (perhaps slightly above average due to the warm reading on Oct 30), we must integrate over the distributions. Because the expected temperature on Nov 16 is close to Nov 9, the probability it is warmer is relatively high, near ~38%. For Dec 9 and Feb 7, the expected temperature is much lower (around 5°C), strongly shifting the distribution downward. Although Feb 7 has a slightly lower expected mean than Dec 9, the normal winter standard deviation is often higher later in the season (which translates into a slightly fatter upper tail), keeping its probability of beating the Nov 9 baseline similar to or slightly above Dec 9. Integrating these distributions gives probabilities of roughly 25-26% for December and February.
